metadata.name: Speckle BIM Integration Database Migration
metadata.purpose: Safe database schema migration for Speckle integration with automated backup and rollback
metadata.author: Claude (Enterprise Integration)

executiveSummary.description: Enterprise-grade database migration with comprehensive safety checks, automated backups, dry-run capability, and post-migration verification. Adds Speckle BIM integration tables, views, triggers, and RLS policies to enable bidirectional BIM data synchronization.
keyChanges: Create speckle_streams table for project-stream mapping
Create speckle_sync_logs table for synchronization audit trail
Add speckle_object_id, element_type, geometry_data columns to construction_elements
Create v_project_speckle_streams view for project statistics
Enable Row Level Security (RLS) on Speckle tables
Create RLS policies for project member access control
prerequisites: Docker CLI available for database operations
Sufficient disk space for backup creation
construction_elements table exists from previous migrations

preFlightChecks.name: Validate migration file exists
preFlightChecks.validationLogic: File must exist at specified path

onFailure.message: Migration file not found: libs/database/migrations/003_speckle_integration.sql

preFlightChecks.name: Validate PostgreSQL connectivity
preFlightChecks.description: Ensure database container is running and accepting connections
preFlightChecks.command: docker exec ectropy-postgres-1 psql -U postgres -c "SELECT version()"

onFailure.message: Cannot connect to PostgreSQL. Is the database running? Check: docker ps

preFlightChecks.name: Check if migration already applied
preFlightChecks.description: Verify speckle_streams table does not already exist
preFlightChecks.command: docker exec ectropy-postgres-1 psql -U postgres -d ectropy_dev -t -c "SELECT EXISTS (SELECT FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema = 'public' AND table_name = 'speckle_streams');"
preFlightChecks.validationLogic: If returns 't', migration may already be applied

onFailure.message: Speckle tables already exist. Migration may have been applied. Continue anyway? (yes/no)

preFlightChecks.name: Validate migration SQL syntax
preFlightChecks.description: Check SQL file contains expected CREATE TABLE statements
preFlightChecks.validationLogic: SQL must contain speckle_streams and speckle_sync_logs table creation

onFailure.message: Expected table creation statements not found in migration file

phases.name: Pre-Migration Safety Checks
phases.description: Validate database connectivity and migration readiness

steps.name: Verify migration file exists
steps.description: Check that 003_speckle_integration.sql is present
steps.command: Test-Path libs/database/migrations/003_speckle_integration.sql
steps.purpose: Ensure migration file is available before proceeding

onFailure.message: Migration file not found: libs/database/migrations/003_speckle_integration.sql

steps.name: Check PostgreSQL connectivity
steps.description: Verify database container is running and accessible
steps.command: docker exec ectropy-postgres-1 psql -U postgres -c "SELECT version()"
steps.purpose: Ensure database is available for migration

onFailure.message: Cannot connect to PostgreSQL. Is the database running? Check: docker ps

steps.name: Check migration status
steps.description: Verify if migration has already been applied
steps.command: docker exec ectropy-postgres-1 psql -U postgres -d ectropy_dev -t -c "SELECT EXISTS (SELECT FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema = 'public' AND table_name = 'speckle_streams');"
steps.purpose: Prevent duplicate migration application

validation.criteria: Should return 'f' if migration not applied

onFailure.message: Speckle tables already exist. Continue anyway? (yes/no)

phases.name: Database Backup Creation
phases.description: Create full database backup before applying migration

steps.name: Create backup directory
steps.description: Ensure backups/migrations directory exists
steps.command: New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path backups/migrations -Force
steps.purpose: Prepare directory for backup file storage

onFailure.message: Failed to create backup directory, but migration can continue

steps.name: Execute pg_dump backup
steps.description: Create timestamped database backup using pg_dump
steps.command: docker exec ectropy-postgres-1 pg_dump -U postgres ectropy_dev > backups/migrations/ectropy_dev_pre_migration_003_$timestamp.sql
steps.purpose: Enable rollback to pre-migration state if needed

validation.criteria: Backup file must be created successfully

onFailure.message: Database backup failed. Migration aborted for safety.

notes: Backup file naming pattern: ectropy_dev_pre_migration_003_YYYYMMDD_HHmmss.sql
Backup includes full database schema and data
Typical backup size: 50-500 KB for development database

steps.name: Verify backup file created
steps.description: Check backup file exists and report size
steps.command: $backupSize = (Get-Item $backupFile).Length / 1KB; Write-Host "Backup created ($([math]::Round($backupSize, 2)) KB)"
steps.purpose: Confirm backup was successfully written to disk

onFailure.message: Backup file verification failed

phases.name: Migration SQL Validation
phases.description: Validate migration SQL structure and safety

steps.name: Validate speckle_streams table creation
steps.description: Check SQL contains CREATE TABLE speckle_streams
steps.command: $sqlContent = Get-Content libs/database/migrations/003_speckle_integration.sql -Raw; $sqlContent -match 'CREATE TABLE.*speckle_streams'
steps.purpose: Ensure migration creates required tables

validation.criteria: SQL must contain speckle_streams table creation

onFailure.message: speckle_streams table creation not found in migration

steps.name: Validate speckle_sync_logs table creation
steps.description: Check SQL contains CREATE TABLE speckle_sync_logs
steps.command: $sqlContent -match 'CREATE TABLE.*speckle_sync_logs'
steps.purpose: Ensure sync logging table is created

validation.criteria: SQL must contain speckle_sync_logs table creation

onFailure.message: speckle_sync_logs table creation not found in migration

steps.name: Check for dangerous DROP statements
steps.description: Warn if migration contains DROP TABLE statements
steps.command: $sqlContent -match 'DROP TABLE'
steps.purpose: Alert operator to potentially destructive operations

validation.criteria: Should not contain DROP TABLE statements

onFailure.message: Migration contains DROP TABLE statements - proceed with caution

steps.name: Count migration statements
steps.description: Count CREATE TABLE, CREATE INDEX, ALTER TABLE, CREATE VIEW statements
steps.command: $createTableCount = ([regex]::Matches($sqlContent, 'CREATE TABLE')).Count; # Similar for other statement types
steps.purpose: Provide migration summary for dry-run mode

onFailure.message: Statement counting failed, but migration can continue

phases.name: Migration Execution
phases.description: Apply migration SQL to database (or dry-run)

steps.name: Execute migration SQL
steps.description: Apply migration to ectropy_dev database
steps.command: docker exec -i ectropy-postgres-1 psql -U postgres -d ectropy_dev < libs/database/migrations/003_speckle_integration.sql
steps.purpose: Create Speckle integration schema in database

onFailure.message: Migration failed. Check logs for errors. Backup available at: $backupFile

steps.name: Record migration duration
steps.description: Calculate and display migration execution time
steps.command: $duration = ((Get-Date) - $migrationStart).TotalSeconds; Write-Host "Migration applied successfully ($([math]::Round($duration, 2))s)"
steps.purpose: Provide performance metrics for migration

onFailure.message: Duration calculation failed, but migration succeeded

phases.name: Post-Migration Verification
phases.description: Verify all schema changes were applied correctly

steps.name: Verify speckle_streams table exists
steps.description: Check speckle_streams table was created
steps.command: docker exec ectropy-postgres-1 psql -U postgres -d ectropy_dev -t -c "SELECT EXISTS (SELECT FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema = 'public' AND table_name = 'speckle_streams');"
steps.purpose: Confirm primary Speckle table creation

onFailure.message: Table 'speckle_streams' not found - migration incomplete

steps.name: Verify speckle_sync_logs table exists
steps.description: Check speckle_sync_logs table was created
steps.command: docker exec ectropy-postgres-1 psql -U postgres -d ectropy_dev -t -c "SELECT EXISTS (SELECT FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema = 'public' AND table_name = 'speckle_sync_logs');"
steps.purpose: Confirm sync logging table creation

onFailure.message: Table 'speckle_sync_logs' not found - migration incomplete

steps.name: Verify v_active_speckle_syncs view exists
steps.description: Check monitoring view was created
steps.command: docker exec ectropy-postgres-1 psql -U postgres -d ectropy_dev -t -c "SELECT EXISTS (SELECT FROM information_schema.views WHERE table_schema = 'public' AND table_name = 'v_active_speckle_syncs');"
steps.purpose: Confirm monitoring view creation

onFailure.message: View 'v_active_speckle_syncs' not found

steps.name: Verify v_project_speckle_streams view exists
steps.description: Check statistics view was created
steps.command: docker exec ectropy-postgres-1 psql -U postgres -d ectropy_dev -t -c "SELECT EXISTS (SELECT FROM information_schema.views WHERE table_schema = 'public' AND table_name = 'v_project_speckle_streams');"
steps.purpose: Confirm statistics view creation

onFailure.message: View 'v_project_speckle_streams' not found

steps.name: Verify speckle_object_id column added
steps.description: Check construction_elements.speckle_object_id column exists
steps.command: docker exec ectropy-postgres-1 psql -U postgres -d ectropy_dev -t -c "SELECT EXISTS (SELECT FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'construction_elements' AND column_name = 'speckle_object_id');"
steps.purpose: Confirm column addition to existing table

onFailure.message: Column 'speckle_object_id' not found in construction_elements

steps.name: Test query execution
steps.description: Execute test query on speckle_streams table
steps.command: docker exec ectropy-postgres-1 psql -U postgres -d ectropy_dev -c "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM speckle_streams;"
steps.purpose: Verify table is queryable and accessible

onFailure.message: Cannot query speckle_streams table - possible permission issue

phases.name: Post-Migration Actions
phases.description: Provide next steps and deployment guidance

steps.name: Display backup location
steps.description: Show operator where backup file is stored
steps.command: Write-Host "Backup Location: $backupFile"
steps.purpose: Inform operator of rollback capability

onFailure.message: Could not display backup location

steps.name: Display next steps
steps.description: Show recommended post-migration actions
steps.command:
Write-Host "Next Steps:
  1. Restart API Gateway
  2. Create Speckle admin user
  3. Run smoke tests"
steps.purpose: Guide operator through post-migration deployment

onFailure.message: Could not display next steps

nextSteps: Restart API Gateway to pick up schema changes: docker-compose restart api-gateway
Create Speckle admin user at http://localhost:8080
Verify Speckle integration in web dashboard BIM viewer
Test bidirectional sync with sample BIM model

databaseSchema.purpose: Real-time monitoring of active synchronization operations
Automatically update updated_at timestamp on row modification

policies.name: speckle_streams_access_policy
policies.using: User can access streams for projects they are member of or owner of

policies.name: speckle_sync_logs_access_policy
policies.using: User can access sync logs for projects they are member of or owner of

dataIntegrity.validation: Migration is purely additive - no data deletion or modification
All foreign keys reference existing tables (projects)
All performance indexes created without errors

performanceThresholds.measurement: Total time from start to completion

triggerConditions: Manual operator decision due to unexpected issues

steps.name: Locate backup file
steps.command: ls -lh backups/migrations/ectropy_dev_pre_migration_003_*.sql
steps.purpose: Find the pre-migration backup with timestamp
notes: Backup files are named: ectropy_dev_pre_migration_003_YYYYMMDD_HHmmss.sql
Use most recent backup if multiple exist
Verify backup file size is reasonable (> 10 KB)

steps.name: Stop dependent services
steps.command: docker-compose stop api-gateway web-dashboard
steps.purpose: Prevent database connections during restore
notes: This prevents active connections from interfering with restore
Services will be restarted after rollback completion

steps.name: Drop Speckle tables
steps.command: docker exec ectropy-postgres-1 psql -U postgres -d ectropy_dev -c "DROP TABLE IF EXISTS speckle_sync_logs CASCADE; DROP TABLE IF EXISTS speckle_streams CASCADE;"
steps.purpose: Remove Speckle tables before full restore
notes: CASCADE ensures dependent objects are removed
This step prevents conflicts during restore

steps.name: Restore from backup
steps.command: docker exec -i ectropy-postgres-1 psql -U postgres ectropy_dev < backups/migrations/ectropy_dev_pre_migration_003_TIMESTAMP.sql
steps.purpose: Restore database to pre-migration state
notes: Replace TIMESTAMP with actual backup timestamp
Restore may take 1-3 minutes depending on database size
All data from backup will be restored

steps.name: Verify table removal
steps.command: docker exec ectropy-postgres-1 psql -U postgres -d ectropy_dev -c "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_name IN ('speckle_streams', 'speckle_sync_logs');"
steps.purpose: Confirm Speckle tables were removed
notes: Expected result: count = 0
If count > 0, tables were not removed - investigate manually

steps.name: Verify column removal from construction_elements
steps.command: docker exec ectropy-postgres-1 psql -U postgres -d ectropy_dev -c "SELECT EXISTS (SELECT FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'construction_elements' AND column_name = 'speckle_object_id');"
steps.purpose: Confirm added columns were removed
notes: Expected result: f (false)
If t (true), column still exists - may need manual cleanup

steps.name: Restart services
steps.command: docker-compose start api-gateway web-dashboard
steps.purpose: Restore application services after rollback
notes: Services should start normally after rollback
Check logs for any errors during startup

steps.name: Test database connectivity
steps.command: docker exec ectropy-postgres-1 psql -U postgres -d ectropy_dev -c "SELECT version();"
steps.purpose: Verify database is operational after rollback
notes: Should return PostgreSQL version
Confirms database is accessible and functional

checks.name: Speckle tables removed
checks.command: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_name LIKE 'speckle_%'

checks.name: Speckle views removed
checks.command: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM information_schema.views WHERE table_name LIKE 'v_%speckle%'

checks.name: construction_elements columns removed
checks.command: SELECT column_name FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'construction_elements' AND column_name IN ('speckle_object_id', 'element_type', 'geometry_data')

verification.successCriteria: All Speckle-related objects removed, database operational, existing data intact

postRollbackActions: Review migration SQL for syntax errors or constraint violations
Check PostgreSQL logs for detailed error messages
Re-run migration with --DryRun flag to test before reapplication

monitoring.duration: First 24 hours after migration critical, then ongoing

keyMetrics.query: SELECT (SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'completed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)::float / COUNT(*)) * 100 FROM speckle_sync_logs
SELECT AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (completed_at - started_at))) FROM speckle_sync_logs WHERE status = 'completed'
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM speckle_sync_logs WHERE status = 'failed' AND started_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '1 day'
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM construction_elements WHERE speckle_object_id IS NOT NULL

alertThresholds.action: Investigate Speckle server connectivity and API configuration
Check if Speckle integration is being used

alertThresholds.condition: Database query errors on Speckle tables
alertThresholds.action: Check schema integrity and RLS policies

dashboards: Sync operation status: SELECT * FROM v_active_speckle_syncs
Project integration overview: SELECT * FROM v_project_speckle_streams
Recent failures: SELECT * FROM speckle_sync_logs WHERE status = 'failed' ORDER BY started_at DESC LIMIT 10
Sync performance: SELECT operation, AVG(objects_processed), AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (completed_at - started_at))) FROM speckle_sync_logs WHERE status = 'completed' GROUP BY operation

successCriteria.criterion: speckle_streams table exists with correct schema
successCriteria.validation: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_name = 'speckle_streams'

successCriteria.criterion: speckle_sync_logs table exists with correct schema
successCriteria.validation: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_name = 'speckle_sync_logs'
SELECT EXISTS (SELECT FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'construction_elements' AND column_name = 'speckle_object_id')
SELECT relname FROM pg_class WHERE relrowsecurity = true AND relname LIKE 'speckle_%'
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_indexes WHERE tablename IN ('speckle_streams', 'speckle_sync_logs', 'construction_elements') AND indexname LIKE '%speckle%'
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM pg_trigger WHERE tgname LIKE '%speckle%updated_at'

successCriteria.criterion: No migration errors in PostgreSQL logs

supportedQueries.returns: Array of migration phases with steps
supportedQueries.purpose: Retrieve ordered migration steps for execution
Assess migration risk and safety measures
Retrieve rollback steps if migration fails

supportedQueries.returns: Complete list of database schema modifications

decisionSupport: Suggest monitoring queries for post-migration health checks
Calculate migration risk based on database size and complexity

knownIssues.title: Migration may fail if construction_elements table missing
knownIssues.impact: Migration aborts with foreign key constraint error
knownIssues.mitigation: Migration script checks for table existence and uses idempotent DO blocks
knownIssues.action: Ensure previous migrations (001, 002) have been applied before running 003

knownIssues.title: Backup creation may fail on low disk space
knownIssues.mitigation: Script checks backup file creation success before proceeding
knownIssues.action: Ensure sufficient disk space (100+ MB free) before migration

knownIssues.title: RLS policies require current_user_id() function
knownIssues.impact: RLS policies may not function if function doesn't exist
knownIssues.mitigation: Migration SQL includes CREATE OR REPLACE for current_user_id() function
knownIssues.action: No action required - migration handles this automatically

optional.name: DRY_RUN
optional.description: Show what would be done without applying changes

usageExamples.description: Execute migration with automatic backup creation
notes: This is the recommended approach for production
Backup will be created automatically
All validation checks will be performed

usageExamples.description: Show what migration would do without applying changes
notes: No database changes are made
Validation checks are still performed
Statement counts are displayed
Use this to test migration safety before applying
Shows detailed progress of each step
Useful for troubleshooting
Backup is created automatically
Only use in development environments
No rollback capability without backup
Faster execution but higher risk

changelog.author: Claude (Enterprise Integration)
changes: Initial runbook creation from PowerShell script
Added comprehensive phase breakdown (6 phases, 23 steps)
Documented all safety checks and validation steps
Added MCP integration queries and decision support
Documented database schema changes and RLS policies
Added monitoring queries and success criteria
Included usage examples for different scenarios
